description_tesim: Postcard with a view of the entrance to Sunset Trail in Santa Monica which leads from the Palisades Park down the cliff to the beach. The trail is bordered by a rustic eucalyptus branch fence. The entrance to the trail is marked by a large wooden sign supported by a wooden framework made of tree trunks. The tops of the cliffs are at right and are covered with an assortment of bushes, and the ocean is visible in the background at left. The Pacific Coast Highway is at the base of the trail in the background at left and is lined with utility poles.
The photograph of Sunset Trail is by the photographer Taylor.

Middle photo: view of the Santa Monica beach looking north from the Palisades Park, ca.1905. The beach is at left and is a long, wide, sandy expanse. In the foreground is a row of short plants with long, pointed leaves. The Palisades walk makes its way up the cliffs at right, and there are large mountains visible across the water at center.

Upper photo: view of a wide dirt road on the face of a cliff in Santa Monica's Palisades Park, 1910-1920. The road is at center and leads from the top of the cliffs at left down to the beach at right. It is lined with a rustic eucalyptus branch fence and several large plants. A tall rock formation can be seen towards the bottom of the road at right. The beach is visible in the distance at center and right. Several tall mountains form a range in the background.

description_tesim: Middle photo: View of the lighthouse on the Santa Monica Bay coast. The lighthouse consists of a long rectangular building with a tall tower at left. The lighthouse was built on the site of the former Long Wharf.

Bottom photo: view facing north towards Palisades Park in Santa Monica (upper right) and a broad beach along the waterline at left. Bordering the beach is the two-lane Pacific Coast Highway, and running parallel to the highway are railroad tracks. Several early-model automobiles are parked near the side of the road on the beach. There are steep cliffs at right, and a narrow road  climbs up the side of them. There are two piers jutting out into the water at center. The shore juts into the water in the distance, and this land is marked by steep mountains rather than cliffs.

Upper photo: View from the north towards of Castle Rock, the Pacific Coast Highway and cliffs. A car is parked on the sand near Castle Rock. Castle rock was leveled in the mid 1950's and is now paved over.

title_tesim: Drawings by Edward Vischer of four California missions [drawings, circa 1870]
alternative_title_tesim: Departed Mission Relics, rescued from oblivion by the strong early-life impressions, astounding memory, descriptive power and untiring obligingness of California's historian General Mar[iano] Guad[alupe] Vallejo. / Santa Cruz 1828, La Purisima in 1824, San Rafael, 1831, San Francisco Solano 1832 / Missions of Santa Cruz, Purissima, San Rafael and San Francisco Solano 1817/30 / Presumable appearance, by elevations from the respective Ground-plans, a most welcome contribution to complete this antiquarian Collection, a lasting memento from an interesting sojourn at Lachryma Montis, the General's congenial, truly arcadian home, October 10 to 15th of 1878.
named_subject_tesim: Mission San Rafael Arcangel (San Rafael, Calif.)
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: C. C. Pierce Photographic Collection,UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library.
description_tesim: Drawings by Edward Vischer of four California missions, and a small oval portrait of the General inset in the caption, circa 1870.

The drawings were re-purposed for Vallejo's project; they are placed within windows cut out of the inscribed upper sheet of paper. Edward Vischer (1809–1878), a German-born painter and photographer, migrated to Mexico at the age of 19. He is best known for his pencil sketches of California landscapes throughout the 1860's and 1870's, especially of missions, trees, mountains and rural scenes. He published his work in Pictorial of California (1870), and two albums of drawings of California missions (1872 and 1878).
combined_subject_ssim: Mission San Rafael Arcangel (San Rafael, Calif.)
